customers.name: Municipal Parking Director
customers.description: Leader responsible for city-owned curbside and off-street parking performance. Accountable for parking revenue, occupancy, policy compliance, and driver experience across mobile, kiosk, permit, and enforcement channels.
careAbout: Net parking yield by zone
Lower leakage and better compliance
Fewer queues and complaints at entry or payment
Operational control across multiple payment channels
Future-proofing for EV, reservations, and enforcement modernization
winsThem: One operating model for tariff setup, sessions, permits, enforcement, and analytics
Digital and hardware channels that stay consistent with city policy
Clear KPI visibility across occupancy, payments, and violations
A migration path that works with existing city systems and assets
theirFear: Revenue leakage from fragmented systems and weak policy execution
Political backlash from poor driver experience or unreliable payments
Long, procurement-heavy programs that never produce visible results

jobsToBeDone.name: Run parking and curb operations as one governed system
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Move from disconnected lots, kiosks, apps, and enforcement tools to a controlled operating model for pricing, payments, access, and compliance.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Higher revenue capture, lower operating friction, and cleaner public accountability across curbside and off-street assets.

steps.step: Configure the parking estate and policies
steps.description: Model zones, rates, permits, exemptions, events, and operating rules across city assets.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Parking Configuration & Policy Management
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Keeps tariffs, permits, sessions, and local rules consistent across channels and locations.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Dynamic Pricing & Occupancy Optimization
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Adjusts rate strategy based on demand, dwell patterns, and utilization goals.

steps.description: Support app, kiosk, permit, reservation, and barrier-based parking flows without fragmented operator control.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Payment Channel Orchestration
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Runs mobile, kiosk, in-car, and partner-originated payment flows through one payment-control layer.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Access, Permit & LPR Control
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Manages entry, permit validation, and LPR-based access or session validation.

steps.description: Connect compliance workflows, evidence, violations, and operator dashboards to continuous optimization.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Enforcement & Compliance Workflows
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Coordinates violation, permit, exemption, and citation workflows with traceable evidence.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Operator Analytics & Control Tower
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Gives parking operators a shared KPI view for yield, occupancy, payment mix, and compliance.

productStrategy.vision: Become the control tower for parking and curb assets so operators can manage pricing, access, compliance, and monetization as one governed system.

milestones: Unified tariff, permit, and zone model across priority parking accounts
Cross-channel payment and access consistency controls
Structured compliance and operator KPI cockpit

3_year.focus: Cross-city optimization and integrated curb operations
3_year.productTheme: Demand-aware parking and curb operating platform
milestones: Dynamic pricing and occupancy recommendations in core city accounts
Shared compliance workflows across more municipal estates
Integrated parking and curb inventory control

5_year.focus: Citywide curb and parking monetization leadership
5_year.productTheme: Mobility infrastructure operating system for parking assets

revenueStreams: Integrated parking, curb, and access monetization

milestones: Unified parking, curb, permit, and enforcement operating model
Portfolio-level city asset monetization analytics
Parking products fully converged across acquired brands where reuse matters

top.name: Net parking yield
top.description: Economic return generated from parking assets after direct operating costs and leakage.

children.name: Paid session conversion rate
children.description: Share of parked vehicles converted into valid paid sessions or permits.

children.name: Occupancy balance by zone
children.description: Degree to which occupancy stays within target levels by location and time.

children.name: Barrier queue time
children.description: Average time vehicles spend waiting at gated access points.

children.name: Violation resolution cycle time
children.description: Time needed to process and close parking violations or permit disputes.

top.name: Parking platform ARR
top.description: Recurring software and services revenue from parking operator accounts.

children.name: Gross revenue retention
children.description: Revenue retained from existing parking operator accounts.

children.name: Reservations and permit attach rate
children.description: Share of parking accounts using higher-value reservation, permit, or access modules.

group: City Authorities and Urban Mobility Planners

customers.name: Urban Mobility Policy Director
customers.description: City leader accountable for mobility policy effectiveness, congestion reduction, curb strategy, and evidence-backed city planning across parking, transport, and public-space interventions.
careAbout: Policy decisions backed by evidence rather than anecdote
Congestion and search-traffic reduction
Better curb allocation and pricing outcomes
Measurable sustainability and livability impact
A coherent citizen mobility experience across city services
winsThem: A shared urban mobility data layer across parking, transit, curb, and demand signals
Scenario analysis that turns policy choices into measurable outcomes
Planning tools that work without replacing every legacy system
theirFear: Data-rich dashboards that do not influence policy decisions
Fragmented city vendors producing disconnected evidence
Political pressure without reliable outcome measurement

jobsToBeDone.name: Turn fragmented mobility signals into policy decisions
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Use operational mobility data to shape pricing, curb, parking, and transport interventions with more confidence.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: City decisions become more defensible, measurable, and easier to iterate over time.

steps.description: Combine parking, permit, occupancy, event, payments, and transport signals into one analysis layer.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: City Mobility Intelligence
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Creates the common data model for mobility operations, curb usage, and policy-grade analytics.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Enterprise Integration Layer
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Connects municipal, operator, enforcement, and transport systems with reusable patterns.

steps.step: Model policy options and monitor outcomes
steps.description: Test pricing, curb, and access interventions against observed behavior and outcome targets.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Planning, Scenario & Outcome Modeling
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Maps interventions to expected demand, congestion, and utilization outcomes.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Operational Data, KPI & Analytics Platform
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides trusted reporting on policy effectiveness and ongoing operational movement.

productStrategy.vision: Make Arrive the evidence and decision layer that helps cities connect parking, curb, and transport operations to better mobility policy outcomes.

1_year.focus: Trustworthy city intelligence and intervention visibility
milestones: Shared city KPI pack spanning parking and transport operations
First intervention and scenario modeling workflows
Reusable city-system ingestion patterns

3_year.focus: Policy-to-operations feedback loop at city scale
3_year.productTheme: Integrated city mobility planning and operating platform
milestones: Decision support connected to live operational metrics
Shared planning layer across parking, curb, and transport interventions
Policy-outcome reporting embedded in account reviews

5_year.focus: Arrive as a citywide mobility intelligence partner
5_year.productTheme: Urban mobility operating and planning infrastructure
milestones: Planning and operations fully joined in shared municipal accounts
Policy effectiveness benchmarking across city portfolios
Arrive positioned as a long-term city mobility infrastructure partner

top.name: Mobility policy effectiveness
top.description: Degree to which city interventions improve movement, livability, and curb usage outcomes.

children.name: Average parking search time
children.description: Time drivers spend searching for parking before finding a valid place to stop.

children.name: Traffic hotspot volume
children.description: Observed vehicle intensity in identified congestion hotspots.

children.name: Policy experiment decision cycle
children.description: Time needed to move from intervention idea to outcome-backed decision.

top.name: Public-sector platform ARR
top.description: Recurring revenue from city and authority accounts using planning, parking, and mobility solutions.

children.name: Cross-solution attach rate
children.description: Share of city accounts using planning plus operational mobility products.

customers.name: Transit Revenue and Operations Executive
customers.description: Executive responsible for fare collection, revenue assurance, device estate, and operational continuity across buses, trams, and regional transport services.
careAbout: Low-friction boarding and payment acceptance
Revenue capture with fewer payment exceptions
Reliable field-device operations
Modernization without transport disruption
winsThem: Open payment that works with existing validation and control environments
Better-fare and payment reporting with fewer manual exceptions
Shared telemetry across devices, transactions, and operations
theirFear: Boarding delays caused by fragile devices or payment failures
A modernization program that breaks service during rollout
High support cost from fragmented payment and exception handling

jobsToBeDone.name: Modernize fare collection without disrupting service
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Introduce open payment, device visibility, and better operational control while protecting throughput and revenue assurance.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Faster boarding, stronger fare capture, and a more supportable transport-payment estate.

steps.description: Run account-based and open-payment journeys across validators, handheld devices, and settlement rails.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Transit Fare & Open Payment Engine
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Handles open payment, fare rules, taps, and capping logic.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Settlement, Ledger & Revenue Reconciliation
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Creates a reconciled financial backbone for transit transactions and exceptions.

steps.step: Operate the field-device and exception estate
steps.description: Track validators, vending, handheld devices, outages, and operational anomalies.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Device & Validator Management
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Monitors transport devices, status, configuration, and maintenance readiness.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Transit Operations Telemetry
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Connects transaction, device, and alert data into one operating view.

productStrategy.vision: Make transit payment modernization operationally safe by combining open payment, device control, and revenue assurance into one dependable transport platform.

milestones: Operational telemetry across validators and payment events
Cleaner transport-payment reconciliation flows
Reliability-first rollout pack for open-payment deployments

3_year.productTheme: Transit revenue and device operations platform
milestones: Standardized open-payment rollout model across more operators
Device lifecycle and maintenance readiness workflows at scale
Shared performance reporting across transit accounts

revenueStreams: Integrated device, settlement, and analytics services

milestones: Fully integrated payment, device, and exception operations stack
Transport modernization playbook reusable across regions
Arrive established as a default partner for open-payment transit rollouts

top.name: Fare system effectiveness
top.description: Operational effectiveness of payment, validation, and fare-collection workflows.

children.name: Average boarding time
children.description: Time required for passengers to board and validate payment.

children.name: Tap success rate
children.description: Share of payment taps completed without error or fallback.

children.name: Fare capture rate
children.description: Share of journeys successfully charged under the intended fare rules.

top.name: Transit platform ARR
top.description: Recurring revenue from transit payment, devices, and operational services.

children.name: Processed payment volume
children.description: Transaction volume processed through transit payment services.

customers.name: Fleet Mobility and Expense Manager
customers.description: Manager accountable for employee, visitor, and vehicle parking policies, spend control, centralized billing, and operational continuity across a distributed fleet or business estate.
careAbout: Parking spend control by team, vehicle, and policy
Less reimbursement and manual claims work
Simpler employee and visitor parking administration
Cross-market control for fleets and business sites
winsThem: Policy-driven parking entitlements and billing controls
Centralized account, cost-center, and expense visibility
theirFear: Parking abuse and policy exceptions that erode control
Poor field-driver experience that creates downtime

jobsToBeDone.name: Control parking spend across employees, visitors, and vehicles
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Apply consistent parking and expense policy while keeping parking access easy enough for operations teams, field workers, and office users.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Lower admin overhead, better policy compliance, and fewer spend surprises across the fleet and workplace estate.

steps.description: Set driver, employee, visitor, and site-based parking entitlements and budget rules.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Fleet & Business Policy Controls
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Defines parking entitlements, business rules, and country or site-level controls.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Visitor & Temporary Access Workflows
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Handles guest, contractor, and short-term parking access without manual coordination.

steps.description: Match sessions, account usage, and policy exceptions to centralized invoicing and reporting.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Business Billing, Tagging & Expense Controls
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Supports centralized invoicing, session notes, and spend allocation by team, trip, or vehicle.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Settlement, Ledger & Revenue Reconciliation
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Keeps billing and partner settlement traceable across usage and payment channels.

productStrategy.vision: Make business and fleet parking feel administratively simple by combining policy, billing, and employee or driver self-service into one controllable product.

milestones: Centralized account billing and tagging controls
Visitor and temporary parking workflow support
Cleaner employee and driver self-service parking paths

3_year.productTheme: Fleet and workplace mobility administration platform

revenueStreams: Integrated expense and parking operations services

milestones: Shared policy model across employee, driver, and visitor parking
Account-level cost and policy analytics by site and market
Deeper integration into business identity and finance systems

5_year.focus: Business mobility platform leadership in managed parking
5_year.productTheme: Operational parking infrastructure for employers and fleets

revenueStreams: Integrated parking, visitor, and fleet operations

milestones: Unified enterprise parking and mobility control layer
Cross-brand business-account convergence where reuse matters
Arrive positioned as the default parking operations layer for distributed businesses

top.name: Mobility productivity gain
top.description: Operational productivity improvement delivered through lower parking administration and faster driver workflows.

children.name: Receipts per employee
children.description: Number of employee reimbursement artifacts requiring manual handling.

children.name: Parking cost per active vehicle
children.description: Average parking spend allocated to an active fleet vehicle.

children.name: Policy exception rate
children.description: Share of parking usage that falls outside defined business policy.

top.name: Business and fleet ARR
top.description: Recurring revenue from fleets, employers, and business mobility accounts.

children.name: Active business accounts
children.description: Number of business customers with live users and usage.

customers.name: Mobility Platform Partner Manager
customers.description: Business and integration leader at a mobility platform, super-app, automotive service, or partner network who wants to connect once and monetize access to parking, transit, or mobility inventory across multiple markets.
careAbout: Fast market onboarding
Stable APIs and certification
Clear settlement and performance reporting
Better attach rates from adjacent mobility services
winsThem: One partner integration model across operators, countries, and mobility service types
Clear operator rules, certification, and sandbox support
Shared analytics on transactions, attach, and market performance
theirFear: Low attach economics after integration effort

jobsToBeDone.name: Integrate once and transact across many operator environments
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Connect partner demand channels to parking, transport, and mobility inventory without bespoke integration per market.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Faster expansion, lower onboarding cost, and healthier network economics.

steps.description: Use APIs, certification, and documentation to start transacting in multiple operator environments.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Partner API, Sandbox & Certification
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides the controlled partner integration surface, test tooling, and launch workflow.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Market Catalog & Operator Rules
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Makes market availability, local rules, and operator-specific constraints explicit.

steps.description: Monitor partner performance, settlement, and adjacent-service attach to prioritize expansion.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Partner Analytics & Revenue Reporting
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Shows transaction success, attach, market mix, and settlement status by partner.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Settlement, Ledger & Revenue Reconciliation
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Reconciles multi-party transactions and monetization events across markets.

productStrategy.vision: Make Arrive the easiest mobility network to integrate with, so partners can launch once, expand faster, and trust the economics across markets.

1_year.focus: Launch speed, rule clarity, and partner trust

supporting: Time to launch a new market

milestones: Reusable certification and onboarding workflow
Market catalog with operator-rule transparency
Partner performance and settlement reporting pack

customerKPI.northStar: Time to launch a new market

milestones: Partner launches productized across core mobility verticals
Automotive and in-car services integrated into the same partner model
Shared commercial and quality analytics across ecosystem accounts

5_year.focus: Arrive as the default integration layer for urban mobility services

supporting: Time to launch a new market
Network density across operators and partners

milestones: One partner operating model spanning parking, transit, and in-car services
Shared settlement and analytics fabric across the ecosystem
Arrive positioned as a core mobility infrastructure partner rather than a single-vertical vendor

top.description: Economic and operational return the partner gets from integrating with Arrive inventory and services.
